events.label: assistant
events.text: Findings written to `/app/answer.json`. Summary: the group lockbox showed classic misuse signs — box left open/unsecured while crew was still on the equipment, missing individual employee locks, isolation keys found outside the box, and no roster/tag identifying who was locked on. This defeats OSHA 1910.147(f)(3)'s core protection for group lockout, so it's a **fail**, **high risk**, requiring immediate stop-work and correction (each worker applies their own lock, keys secured inside the box, documentation completed) before work resumes.
